title: Berry Stain on Carpet
stainChemistry: Berry on carpet is the same anthocyanin tannin chemistry as on fabric — blueberry, blackberry, raspberry, strawberry, mulberry, cranberry. Blueberry and blackberry carry the heaviest pigment load. On carpet, the stain moves deeper with pooled liquid and wicks back up as the carpet dries if the pad gets contaminated. Nylon, olefin, polyester face yarn tolerate acid-side spotters and 3% H₂O₂; wool narrows the lane sharply. The universal-berry-on-carpet enemy is alkaline cleaners (ammonia, OxiClean paste, "all-purpose" sprays) which permanently lock the anthocyanin as a darker brown.
whyThisWorks: Same extraction-beats-chemistry logic as red wine on carpet, adapted for berry: cold NSD via damp cloth, acid-side tannin spotter for the anthocyanin bond, 3% H₂O₂ for oxidation. Weighted dry towel between every wet pass protects the pad. Never alkaline, never pour, never rub.
defaultAssumption: Assume synthetic face yarn unless user says wool. Wool + berry = pro-lane. Fresh + synthetic = solid DIY odds; aged dark-berry (blueberry, blackberry) on light carpet often plateaus at faint shadow.

spottingProtocol.technique: Press dry, lift, repeat — no rubbing
spottingProtocol.equipment: Clean white cotton towels, firm pressure
spottingProtocol.instruction: Press a folded white towel straight down with firm pressure. Lift, dry section, press again. Outside-in. Never rub — rubbing drives berry into the pile and pad.

spottingProtocol.equipment: NSD in cold water, damp cloth, wet/dry vac or weighted dry towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Apply cold NSD via damp cloth — press, lift, rotate. Never pour. Extract between passes with wet/dry vac or weighted dry towel. 2–4 cycles.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A) — acid-side tannin treatment
spottingProtocol.instruction: Acid-side tannin spotter by dropper on residual color. Never pour. Dwell 5–10 min. Blot with a dry towel. Patch-test hidden area first.
3% H₂O₂ drop-by-drop. Check every 5 min. Blot dry. One oxidizer at a time; NSD-damp-flush between any repeat.

spottingProtocol.equipment: NSD damp cloth, heavy dry towel + weight
spottingProtocol.instruction: NSD-damp cloth to clear residual chemistry. Weighted dry towel 30 min pulls moisture up from backing before it migrates into pad.
Fan the spot. Don't walk on it. Inspect next day for wicking — if ring returns, pad is contaminated and pro extraction is the call.

steps: 2. Cold NSD via damp cloth, extract between every pass.
3. Acid-side tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter / PRENETT-A) by dropper. 5–10 min.
5. NSD-damp flush + 30 min weighted dry towel.
6. Fan-dry. Inspect next day for wicking.
products: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A)
Wet/dry vac or weighted dry towels
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold throughout — hot water accelerates anthocyanin browning + activates backing adhesive
warnings: Never pour or pool liquid on carpet — pad contamination
Never use alkaline on anthocyanin — locks as permanent brown
Never use hot water — sets tannin brown
Never skip extraction between wet passes

homeSolutions.instruction: Find a hidden patch of the same carpet — inside a closet or under furniture. Dab a drop of cool water + Dawn on it with a white cloth. Wait 2 min. If the cloth picks up carpet dye, your carpet's color isn't stable and home chemistry will lighten it. Skip to step 7 (call a pro).

Berry is anthocyanin — same pigment family as red wine. Alkaline products — ammonia, baking soda, OxiClean paste, most all-purpose sprays — LOCK berry color into carpet as permanent brown. Use ONLY: cold water, Dawn dish soap, 3% hydrogen peroxide.

Press a thick stack of white paper towels or folded white cloth straight down on the berry spot. Lift, move to dry section, press again. Outside-in. Rubbing is the #1 home failure — it drives the berry into the pad.

homeSolutions.agent: Cold Water + Dawn, Damp Cloth Only
homeSolutions.instruction: Dampen a white cloth with cool water + a drop of Dawn. Press onto the spot, lift, rotate. Never pour water on carpet — it sinks to the pad and rings back up. Keep your cloth damp, not dripping. 2–3 cycles.

Mix 1 tbsp Dawn + 1 cup 3% hydrogen peroxide. Dip a white cloth, wring nearly dry, dab the stain. Dwell 5–10 min. Blot with damp cloth. 2–3 cycles on heavy berry. Skip if your patch test came back positive for dye transfer.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia. Patch test first on dark or patterned carpet.

homeSolutions.agent: Weighted Dry Towel Between Every Pass
homeSolutions.instruction: After each wet cycle, put a thick DRY towel on the spot with a book or pan on top for 20–30 min. Pulls moisture UP and out of the pad. Skip this and the stain wicks back as it dries.

homeSolutions.agent: Fan-Dry, Inspect Next Day, Escalate If Needed
homeSolutions.instruction: Fan the area. Don't walk on it until bone-dry. Next day, inspect in daylight. If a faint ring returns, call a carpet cleaner. If the carpet is wool, the patch test failed, or two home rounds plateau, also call a pro. Tell them: "Berry on [nylon / wool / synthetic] carpet, age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + 3% peroxide + weighted towel. [Ring wicked back / plateau / patch test failed]."

steps: 1. Patch-test a hidden area with Dawn + 3% peroxide.
2. NO alkaline cleaners on berry — ammonia/OxiClean/baking soda permanently lock it brown.
3. Blot straight down from outside to center. Never rub.
4. Cold water + Dawn on a DAMP cloth — never pour.
5. Dawn + 3% peroxide solution, dab with damp cloth, 5–10 min. 2–3 cycles.
6. Weighted dry towel 20–30 min between every wet pass.
7. Fan-dry, inspect next day. Escalate for wool, wicking, or plateau.
products: Clean white towels / thick paper towel stack
A heavy book or pan for weighted drying
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if wool carpet, stain wicks back as carpet dries, patch test showed dye bleed, or two home rounds plateau.
warnings: NEVER alkaline cleaners on berry — permanent brown
NEVER skip weighted extraction between wet passes
NEVER mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia
NEVER use hot water — sets tannin brown

neverDo: Never pour liquid on carpet — pad contamination
Never alkaline on berry — permanent brown
fiberSensitivities: Nylon / olefin / polyester: tolerates NSD, acid-side spotters, 3% H₂O₂

escalation.when: Wool carpet, stain wicks back, patch test showed dye bleed, or two home rounds plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Berry on [nylon / wool / synthetic] carpet, age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + 3% H₂O₂ + weighted towel."

contextQuestions: Nylon, olefin, polyester, or wool carpet?
What berry — blueberry / blackberry (heavy anthocyanin) or strawberry (lighter)?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Dark-berry (blueberry / blackberry) on light carpet = hardest. Walked-on fresh spill often means pad is contaminated = escalate.
